channel.title: Asha.News
channel.description: AI-powered news analysis, bias detection, and story clustering.

item.title: Hundreds of millions at risk from Chinese shopping app malware
Russian authorities detain suspect over St. Petersburg cafe blast
READ: Trump indictment related to hush money payment
Trump pleads not guilty to 34 felony counts
Utah governor says alleged Kirk shooter not cooperating with authorities
item.description: Gov. Spencer Cox, R-Utah, said Sunday that the suspect in the shooting that killed conservative activist Charlie Kirk is not cooperating with authorities.

item.title: Former boxing world champion Hatton dies aged 46
item.description: Former world champion Ricky Hatton is found dead at his home in Manchester.

item.title: What Time Will ‘Call The Midwife’ Season 14 Be on Netflix?
item.description: And away we go!

item.title: FanDuel promo code: Bet $5, get $300 in bonus bets if your bet wins for Giants vs. Cowboys
item.description: FanDuel Sportsbook is offering a welcome bonus to new customers who have yet to create an account with the site: Bet $5, get $300 in bonus bets if your bet wins!

item.title: Buffalo Bills visit the NY Jets in Week 2—Here’s how to watch for free
item.description: The Bills are the Jets' first AFC East opponent this season.

item.title: NY Giants face rival Cowboys in Week 2—Here’s how to watch for free
item.description: The Giants take on their second divisional rival in two weeks.

item.title: Police seek man who called for Keir Starmer to be ‘assassinated’ at far-right rally
item.description: Met police say further arrests expected after 24 were detained during ‘Unite the Kingdom’ march in London Police are searching for a man who called for Keir Starmer to be killed during Saturday’s “Unite the Kingdom” rally in London, with further arrests expected in the coming days after outbreaks of violence. Between 110,000 and 150,000 were estimated by police to have attended the far-right event, organised by the activist known as Tommy Robinson, and which was described by a cabinet minister on Sunday as a “klaxon call” for the government. Continue reading...

item.title: Britain will ‘never surrender flag’ to far-right protesters, Starmer says
item.description: Exclusive: PM condemns attacks against police, racist intimidation of minorities and use of flag to instil fear Britain will “never surrender” to far-right protesters who use the flag as cover for violence and to instil fear, Keir Starmer has said, condemning attacks against police officers and the racist intimidation of minorities. Starmer said the St George’s flag “represents our diverse country” and that he would not tolerate people being “intimidated on our streets because of their background or the colour of their skin”. Continue reading...

item.title: Man on the run after house explosion injures four
item.description: Man, woman and child in neighbouring houses were injured and Jacob Ryan is wanted by police.

item.title: Tommy Robinson tapping into disquiet in the country, says minister
item.description: Business Secretary Peter Kyle says Robinson has been able to tap into a years-long sense of grievance in the country.

item.title: Scientists extract silver from e-waste using cooking oil
item.description: New research shows how fatty acids in cooking oil can safely dissolve and recover silver from circuit boards without harmful chemicals or environmental damage.

item.title: Musk call for Brits to ‘fight back or die’ denounced by minister
item.description: Tech billionaire’s comments to London march organised by far-right activist Tommy Robinson were ‘totally inappropriate’

item.title: Rangers vs. Mets prediction: MLB odds, picks, best bets for Sunday
item.description: Nolan McLean will help lead the Mets past the Rangers on Sunday afternoon at Citi Field.

item.title: Rubio arrives in Israel amid tensions over strike on Hamas leaders in Qatar
item.description: US secretary of state says Trump ‘not happy’ about Israeli attack that targeted Hamas officials in Doha for Gaza talks The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, has arrived in Israel seeking to mend a rift with Washington’s other allies in the region over Israel’s attempted assassination of Hamas leaders in Qatar and the accelerated expansion of settlements in the occupied West Bank. In talks with the Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, Rubio will try to balance criticism of the Israeli airstrike on a Doha building, which killed aides to a Hamas leader and a Qatari security officer, with a message of overall support for Israel before the expected formal recognition of Palestine by a number of other US allies, including the UK, France, Canada, Australia and Belgium. Continue reading...

item.title: Ukrainian drones strike a Russian top oil refinery, sparking massive fire
item.description: Ukrainian drones overnight struck one of Russia’s largest oil refineries, sparking a fire, according to Russian officials and Ukraine’s military.

item.title: NJ judge rejects bid to free Colt’s Neck quadruple murder suspect Paul Caneiro over trial delay
item.description: A New Jersey judge Friday blocked accused quadruple murderer Paul Caneiro’s bid to be released from jail after he argued his rights were violated by keeping him locked up for over six years without trial. Caneiro, 58 — who is accused of killing his business partner brother and the sibling’s family in Colt’s Neck 2018...

item.title: Scale of ‘Unite the Kingdom’ march shows free speech ‘alive and well’ in UK, says minister – UK politics live
item.description: Peter Kyle said he was not ‘disturbed’ by huge Tommy Robinson-organised far-right protest but condemns violence against the police This report was written by my colleagues Peter Walker, Eleni Courea and Pippa Crerar: Keir Starmer has been warned that time is running out to repair his faltering leadership, with Labour MPs beginning to ask whether he could be challenged as prime minister. Continue reading...

item.title: LIVE: Manchester City vs Manchester United – Premier League
item.description: Follow the build-up, analyses, and live text commentary of the game as City host local rivals United at Etihad Stadium.

item.title: NFL Week 2 picks odds: Seahawks vs. Steelers, Eagles vs. Chiefs
item.description: Our best bets for Sunday's Seahawks-Steelers game and the Chiefs welcoming the Eagles.

item.title: Nepal PM seeks calm, promises to meet protesters’ push to ‘end corruption’
item.description: Sushila Karki asks country to come together to rebuild in first public statement since becoming interim leader.

item.title: Boxing champ Ricky Hatton dead at 46, months before his scheduled comeback
item.description: LONDON – British boxer Ricky Hatton has died at the age of 46, the BBC reported on Sunday. Former world champion Hatton won titles at light-welterweight and welterweight, before retiring in 2012. He had been due to make a comeback for an event in Dubai later this year. Former boxing world champion Ricky Hatton has...

item.title: Georgia freezes bank accounts of parents of jailed former aide to de facto leader
item.description: Move made on same day as Guardian published interview with ex-aide’s mother who criticised Bidzina Ivanishvili The mother of a jailed former aide to Georgia’s de facto leader, Bidzina Ivanishvili has had her bank accounts frozen after she publicly accused Ivanishvili of pursuing a vendetta against her son. The prosecutor’s office in Georgia acted against Marina Ramazashvili and her husband, Alexander, on the same day that the Guardian published an interview with her in which she criticised the billionaire and called for help from the west. Continue reading...

item.title: Yankees’ Jazz Chisholm backing up big words with big play: ‘the best team in the league
item.description: Jazz Chisholm Jr. didn’t just make a statement after the game, when the confident second baseman again called the Yankees “the best team in the league.”

item.title: Ne-Yo Reveals One Requirement He Has in His Relationship With His Four Girlfriends
item.description: Being in a relationship with multiple people sounds exhausting. Taking care of four different people, their needs and emotions, in addition to yourself, that’s a lot of bandwidth being used. However, it’s something that Ne-Yo seems happy to take part in. He’s in a polyamorous relationship with four different women: Cristina “Pretty Baby,” Arielle Hill […] The post Ne-Yo Reveals One Requirement He Has in His Relationship With His Four Girlfriends appeared first on VICE.

item.title: Prince Andrew needs to be fully investigated, Giuffre family says
item.description: Virginia Guiffre, one of Jeffrey Epstein's most prominent victims, alleged she was abused by the prince three times.

item.title: Romania becomes second Nato country to report Russian drone in its airspace
item.description: Ukraine's President Zelensky called the incursion "an obvious expansion of the war by Russia".

item.title: Ricky Hatton, former boxing world champion, dies aged 46
item.description: Hatton, known throughout his career as the Hitman, was one of the best-known British fighters of his generation The British former world boxing champion Ricky Hatton has been found dead at his home at the age of 46. Hatton, known throughout his career as the Hitman, was one of the best-known British fighters of his generation and won several world titles. He retired in 2012 and went on to speak openly about his struggles with depression and alcohol. Continue reading...

item.title: From Zapruder to smartphones: assassination footage reshapes America’s view of political violence
item.description: Presidential historian Tevi Troy explains how social media has transformed public consumption of political violence footage following Charlie Kirk's killing at Utah Valley University.

item.title: Racism or celebration? What England’s flag-hoisters are saying – and what others are hearing
item.description: Speaking to those who are uneasy with the proliferation of flags and those who support it is a journey of discovery If it started anywhere it was among the suburban streets off a large roundabout in Weoley Castle, known by local people in this part of south-west Birmingham’s urban sprawl as “the square”. From Falkirk to Folkestone, Harwich to Holyhead, fluttering off the Angel of the North and marked out on a Wiltshire white horse, the national colours of England and the United Kingdom, and Scotland and Wales to a lesser extent, have been on show across the country in recent weeks. Continue reading...

item.title: How the US rightwing is taking over news media and choking press freedom
item.description: Takeover of broadcast companies by Donald Trump’s allies is harbinger of media capitulation to authoritarian leanings The US right has appeared to increase its influence on mainstream media in America in recent weeks, especially in television news which has been a major target of the Donald Trump administration. CBS News – once home to legends of US journalism like Walter Cronkite and Edward R Murrow – installed a Trump ally as its ombudsman, weeks after the family of Larry Ellison, one of the world’s richest men, and a friend of the US president, sealed control over Paramount, the owner of CBS. Continue reading...

item.title: Utah campus concealed carry permit under fresh scrutiny after Kirk shooting
item.description: HB 128, passed in August, allows people with the proper permit to openly carry firearms on college campuses As authorities at the federal and state levels parse the details of the fatal shooting of far-right activist Charlie Kirk at a university in Utah, a recently passed state bill that allows people with concealed-carry permits to carry firearms on college campuses has drawn fresh scrutiny. Utah has allowed for permitless open and concealed carry of weapons since 2021. But before the passage of HB 128, firearms had to be concealed when carried on college campuses. The law allowed people with the proper permit to carry them openly. Continue reading...

item.title: Train stabbing suspect’s family has history of crimes, records show
item.description: The Brown family's criminal history spans generations, with the latest case involving Decarlos Brown Jr.'s federal charge for allegedly stabbing a Ukrainian commuter to death.

item.title: Trump gets it: Prayer isn’t the problem. Silencing it is
item.description: Trump's promise to safeguard prayer in schools aims to restore religious liberty that has been misunderstood under First Amendment interpretations.

item.title: BetMGM Promo Code NYPDM1600: Get a 20% First Deposit Match up to $1,600 for Jets vs. Bills Week 2
item.description: New bettors can use BetMGM promo code NYPDM1600 to get a 20% first deposit match up to $1,600 in sports bonus for Jets vs. Bills.

item.title: How Emmy nominee Michelle Williams finally found happiness after the tragic death of love Heath Ledger
item.description: Michelle Williams has a “spine made of stainless steel,” a source told Page Six, of the "Dying For Sex" star's long road to happiness.

item.title: Medics in southern Gaza sound alarm over wave of newly displaced Palestinians
item.description: Doctors at Nasser medical complex say it will not be able to cope with large numbers of people fleeing Gaza City Doctors and medical staff at the largest hospital still functioning in Gaza say they will be overwhelmed by a wave of new wounded and sick patients if hundreds of thousands of Palestinians flee the north of the devastated territory in the face of an intensifying Israeli offensive. Dr Mohammed Saqr, the director of nursing at the Nasser medical complex near Khan Younis, in the south of Gaza, said there were not enough staff to cope with even existing demand and that supplies of medicine and fuel were running low. Continue reading...

item.title: Giants vs. Cowboys: Preview, prediction, what to watch for in Week 2
item.description: An inside look at Sunday’s Giants-Cowboys NFL Week 2 matchup at AT&T Stadium:

item.title: JONATHAN TURLEY: Charlie Kirk wouldn't fire people who hated him, he'd win them over
item.description: The assassination of Charlie Kirk highlights free speech hypocrisy, as calls to fire those celebrating his death contradict the very principles he fought for.

item.title: Dozens arrested after anti-immigration protesters scuffled with UK police
item.description: More than two dozen far-right demonstrators were arrested after scuffling with police and anti-racism counter-protesters

item.title: Teacher allegedly showed Charlie Kirk assassination video to 10 and 11-year-olds, told students he deserved to be killed
item.description: A public school teacher has been suspended after allegedly showing a video of Charlie Kirk’s assassination to students as young as 10 in his class, authorities in Canada said. The teacher also said that Kirk deserved to be killed, and gave the young students a speech about anti-fascism and transgender issues, according to a report....

item.title: Crocodile wrestling influencer flouts wildlife rules
item.description: American influencer and self-styled 'Real Tarzann' is being investigated for illegally wrestling wild crocodiles in northern Australia. Critics say animals are suffering to create viral content.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV turns 70 and thanks God, his parents and all those who prayed for him
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has marked his 70th birthday by thanking God, his parents and all those who prayed for him

item.title: Jets vs. Bills: Preview, prediction, what to watch for in Week 2
item.description: An inside look at Sunday’s Jets-Steelers NFL Week 2 matchup at MetLife Stadium:

item.title: ‘Shifting Gears’ Showrunner Wants Jennifer Coolidge To Join Tim Allen’s ABC Sitcom
item.description: From Lotus to lug nuts? Michelle Nader is manifesting Coolidge.

item.title: Jets vs. Bills prediction: NFL Week 1 player props, odds, picks
item.description: The Jets are looking to build on a surprisingly strong offensive showing despite a Week 1 loss against the Steelers.

item.title: Whatever happened to .... the 12-year-old Ukrainian girl in need of a heart transplant?
item.description: During a Russian attack, a medical team drove to extract the heart of a young girl who'd just died and bring it to their hospital, where a 12-year-old was in desperate need of a transplant.

item.title: Mandelson should never have been ambassador, says Epstein victim's family
item.description: In an exclusive interview on Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg, Virginia Giuffre's brother said, "he should never have been given the position".

item.title: Far-right AfD forecast to make gains as North Rhine-Westphalia goes to polls
item.description: Elections in Germany’s most populous state pose first significant test at ballot box for Friedrich Merz as chancellor Germany’s far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD)party is expected to make significant gains as voters in the country’s most populous state go to the polls in local elections that are seen as Friedrich Merz’s first significant electoral test since he took office as chancellor four months ago. North Rhine-Westphalia, home to almost a quarter of Germany’s citizens and made up of swathes of agricultural land, post-industrial towns and cities with large multi-ethnic and student populations, is viewed as something of a bellwether for the country as a whole. Continue reading...

item.title: Russian drone incursion into Poland ‘was Kremlin test on Nato’
item.description: Polish foreign minister says Vladimir Putin seeks to test west’s reactions without triggering full-scale war Poland’s foreign minister, Radosław Sikorski, has said last week’s Russian drone incursion into Polish airspace was an attempt by the Kremlin to test Nato’s reactions by incremental escalations without prompting a full-scale response. Sikorski confirmed that while the drones used in the incursion were capable of carrying ammunition, those that reached Poland were not loaded with explosives. “Interestingly, they were all duds, which suggests to me that Russia tried to test us without starting a war,” Sikorski told the Guardian in Kyiv. Continue reading...

item.title: Mets bullpen ruins Carlos Mendoza’s decision to pull Brandon Sproat early
item.description: The rookie righty worked six scoreless innings in his first home start.

item.title: Epstein birthday book renews questions about links to rich and powerful men
item.description: Book compiled by Ghislaine Maxwell contains multiple innuendo-laden letters – including one from Donald Trump After House Democrats released a scrapbook gifted to Jeffrey Epstein for his 50th birthday, questions have emerged about whether the late child-sex trafficker’s proclivities were an open secret or, at the very least, rumored in the circle of powerful and rich people that surrounded him. Indeed, the so-called birthday book, which was compiled by Epstein accomplice Ghislaine Maxwell, contains multiple letters that are laden with sexual innuendo – including one alleged missive from Donald Trump. Continue reading...

item.title: Charlie Kirk’s death raises fears of ‘beginning of a darker chapter’ for US violence
item.description: Social media and widespread availability of lethal weapons make this era more dangerous than the 1960s – and the violence may increase Charlie Kirk’s killing came amid a rise in political violence in the US, the kind now so frequent that it moves swiftly out of news cycles it would once have dominated. The list is long and growing. From the two assassination attempts on Donald Trump during his campaign last year to Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro’s home burnt in an arson attack in April and the Democratic Minnesota state lawmaker and her husband gunned down by a man dressed as a police officer in June, to name a few. Continue reading...

item.title: Florida vaccine mandate rollback falters after Trump criticism
item.description: Surgeon general Joseph Ladapo retreats on his plans after the US president says: ‘You have vaccines that work’ It took barely two days, and some huffs of disapproval from the White House, before Joseph Ladapo’s bold plan to eliminate all vaccine mandates in Florida began to deflate. The action, the fervently religious state surgeon general had asserted at a fire and brimstone press conference in Tampa, came straight from the heavens: “Who am I, as a man standing here now, to tell you what you should put in your body?” he opined, framing the freedom to reject vaccines as a “reflection of God’s light against the darkness of tyranny and oppression”. Continue reading...

item.title: Charlie Kirk's bold legacy lives on and will shape America for years to come
item.description: A pastor and lawyer shares a personal tribute to Charlie Kirk, reflecting on their friendship and Kirk's lasting impact on conservative politics and young Americans.

item.title: The west is buried under red tape
item.description: A regulatory ratchet is smothering business, households and government

item.title: Brookfield in talks to buy US landlord from Singapore’s GIC in $10bn deal
item.description: Purchase of Yes! Communities would be one of sector’s largest takeovers since 2022

item.title: Giants vs. Cowboys prediction: Week 1 NFL player props
item.description: The Giants are in a must-win spot against the Cowboys on Sunday.

item.title: DAVID MARCUS: Democrats must stop their deadly anti-fascism cosplay
item.description: Charlie Kirk's assassination highlights how years of leftist rhetoric labeling him a fascist contributed to his death by a shooter with leftist ideology.

item.title: Yankees-Aroldis Chapman playoff collision looking likely
item.description: For the past several weeks, the Yankees and Red Sox have been set up to face each other in the wild-card round in just over two weeks.

item.title: Nepal's interim PM vows to end corruption
item.description: Former chief justice, Sushila Karki, urged "calm and cooperation" after days of violent protests. Authorities said at least 72 people were killed in anti-corruption protests that led to the ousting of government.

item.title: Has Israel gone rogue?
item.description: Marc Lamont Hill discusses Israel’s strike on Doha and what it means for Gaza ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: Netanyahu is only obstacle to bringing hostages home, families say
item.description: Their intervention comes as US Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrives in Israel to meet the Israeli PM.

item.title: Public Schools Are Trampling Religious Liberty and Failing Our Kids
item.description: We must change laws and policies, but more importantly, we must change hearts and minds.

item.title: MSNBC’s Shameful Breaking News Coverage on Charlie Kirk
item.description: The subsequent removal of one pundit does not make things right.

item.title: The Western Canon Can Help Us Resist the Totalitarian Temptation
item.description: C. S. Lewis offered a moral and intellectual antidote to the totalitarian temptation: a return to ancient truths to help defeat the lies of his own day, and of ours.

item.title: The GOP Shouldn’t Give Up the Obamacare Fight (Again)
item.description: Don’t let Democrats portray Covid-era subsidies as the new normal.

item.title: ‘Access’ Trumps Safety in New Abortion Shield Laws
item.description: Allowing abortion pill prescriptions to be shipped without a patient’s name will contribute to safety risks.

item.title: Leaders gather for Arab-Islamic summit in Qatar after Israel’s Doha attack
item.description: Qatar seeks a collective response to Israel, which has attacked several other countries as it pummels Gaza.

item.title: Rubio arrives in Israel as Israeli strikes intensify in northern Gaza
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in Israel on Sunday, as Israel intensified its attacks against northern Gaza.

item.title: How the diamond engagement ring was invented – and sold around the world
item.description: The story of the diamond industry is one of monopolies, marketing and a month's salary or more.

item.title: Israeli army carries out its latest ground incursion in southern Syria
item.description: Israel has repeatedly entered Syria since the fall of al-Assad and has carried out air raids across the country.

item.title: Israel’s Gaza offensive sparks fierce clash over Ultra-Orthodox military draft
item.description: Israel faces growing tensions as the Gaza war strains IDF reservists and reignites a fierce debate over Ultra-Orthodox draft exemptions, testing Netanyahu’s government and Israel’s unity.

item.title: King Charles planning royal spectacle for President Trump, with carriage rides and star-studded guest list
item.description: King Charles III will host President Trump and Melania Trump at Windsor Castle for a three-day state visit featuring ceremonial events and a state banquet.

item.title: Larry Ellison’s heir takes aim at Warner Bros and at reshaping Hollywood
item.description: Skydance founder prepares bid just weeks after closing Paramount acquisition

item.title: Mexico's first female president completes first year with high approval, but challenges loom
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has maintained remarkably high marks despite a spate of domestic and international challenges, the biggest among them President Trump.

item.title: Trump took a page out of Nixon's playbook for his attack on Harvard. Only Nixon faced consequences.
item.description: Trump threatened Harvard with a plan to withhold $2.2 billion in federal research funding. Here's why a judge ruled that illegal.

item.title: One of voters’ most powerful tools is under consistent attack from state legislatures
item.description: The Fairness Project says lawmakers across 15 states have filed 148 bills this year designed to make citizen-led ballot initiatives more difficult to pass.

item.title: After Missouri, what state is next? A redistricting race started by Trump continues
item.description: First Trump got Texas to redistrict to help Republicans win five House seats there. California Democrats are trying to counter that. Now Missouri lawmakers move to help the GOP win another seat.

item.title: Iran’s Troubles Are About to Get Worse
item.description: A return to war with Israel is unlikely. But pressure on the economy, and the regime, is about to go up fast.

item.title: Victim of 'racially aggravated' rape thanks community for support
item.description: The victim says she "would never wish this on anyone" and is grateful for the community's support.

item.title: Palestinians flee Israeli bombing of Gaza City to ‘unknown’ in al-Mawasi
item.description: As Israel displaces thousands from the north of the enclave, concerns are growing about conditions in the south.

item.title: LIVE: India vs Pakistan – Asia Cup 2025
item.description: Follow our live build-up, on-ground updates, score, text and photo commentary stream from the cricket match in Dubai.

item.title: Strong winds and heavy rain forecast as stormy weather to hit the UK
item.description: Strong winds and heavy rain forecast as stormy weather to hit the UK. Met Office wind warning issued for Sunday night and Monday.

item.title: Rubio lands in Israel ahead of Netanyahu meeting on Hamas war, Gaza City operation
item.description: Rubio is scheduled to meet one-on-one with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu before the two visit the Western Wall at 2 p.m. local time.

item.title: China’s military warns Philippines against provocations in South China Sea
item.description: Beijing issues warning after joint exercises involving the US and Japan as it pushes a nature reserve plan in the area.

item.title: Trump is making a state visit to the U.K., the homeland of his immigrant mother
item.description: President Trump's U.K. state visit this week won't include the Scottish island where his late immigrant mother was born. Mary Anne MacLeod was a Gaelic speaker who learned English as a second language.

item.title: Brazilian musician identified as victim of 1976 killing by Argentina military
item.description: The bossa nova pianist Francisco Tenório Cerqueira Júnior went out for cigarettes after a concert in Buenos Aires – a forensic team has finally revealed his fate Early on 18 March 1976, Francisco Tenório Cerqueira Júnior, a Brazilian pianist who had played alongside some of Latin America’s greatest musicians, disappeared from the streets of Buenos Aires. For nearly 50 years, his fate has remained a mystery, sparking desperate searches, raising suspicions of government complicity, and inspiring international documentaries. Now the mystery has been solved, with forensic scientists formally identifying Tenório Júnior’s body – and confirming he was a victim of Argentina’s bloody dictatorship. Continue reading...

item.title: Israeli forces arrest young Palestinian woman during raid in Hebron
item.description: Israeli forces arrested a Palestinian student, Baraa Jamal Karama, during a raid on her home in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: Venezuela and the US’s ‘failed’ war on drugs
item.description: The so-called 'war on drugs' has been called ‘a great imperial hypocrisy’ that allows the US to pressure Latin America.

item.title: US, China kick off talks on trade, TikTok, in Madrid meeting
item.description: A team of senior officials from the US and China is huddling in Madrid to discuss trade irritants and a nearing TikTok deadline. Meanwhile, China has announced dumping and discrimination probes into US chips.

item.title: Israel intensifies Gaza strikes, issues threat to residents to move south
item.description: Thousands remain in Gaza City, citing overcrowded shelters and unsafe conditions in the south.

item.title: FBI’s Patel faces US congressional hearings after Kirk death probe missteps
item.description: Fellow conservatives question FBI chief's qualification to head agency as he braces for Senate and House inquiry.

item.title: The prison economy: Behind bars, everyone’s making money
item.description: The world spends hundreds of billions yearly to punish offenders, with little return beyond more crime and less hope. DW examines how private contractors, organized crime gangs and inmates exploit prison for profit.

item.title: Rare ladybird recorded in the UK for the first time
item.description: The Little Arboreal Ladybirds have made their way to the UK across the English Channel.

item.title: Cricket: Hundreds of India vs Pakistan Asia Cup tickets unsold
item.description: Fans and experts blame hot weather and lingering tensions between both nations for unusual ticket sales.

item.title: Trump's top diplomat seeks answers from Israel on way forward in Gaza after Qatar strike
item.description: Donald Trump's top diplomat has arrived in the Middle East amid tensions over Israel's attack on Hamas inside Qatar that has upended efforts to broker an end to the Gaza war.
